body_markdown:
## **A Personal Message From Your Faculty**

**Congratulations.** You've just taken one of the most important steps in your professional life.

You're here because you're ready for something more. More impact. More freedom. More control over your income and your future. You've built a successful career, but you know your expertise deserves to reach beyond the confines of your current role.

Over the next four weeks, you're going to discover something powerful: the frameworks, skills, and confidence to turn your decades of experience into a thriving coaching practice that serves you—not the other way around.

## **What Makes This Program Different**

**This isn't another coaching course.** Most programs teach theory to beginners. We teach practical mastery to proven leaders.

**You're not starting from zero.** You already have the credibility, experience, and business acumen that takes others years to develop. We're simply giving you the coaching methodology to monetize that expertise at £1K-£5K per client, per month.

**We've done this before.** Our faculty has helped hundreds of executives make this exact transition. We know the challenges you'll face, the doubts you'll have, and exactly how to guide you through them.

**Real results, real fast.** While others spend months on theory, you'll be practicing with real frameworks from Day 1. By Week 4, you'll be certified and ready to start earning.

## **Your Journey: From Executive to Elite Coach**

**Where you are now:** Successful in your field, but feeling constrained by corporate politics, limited income growth, and lack of true autonomy.

**Where you're going:** Equipped with world-class coaching skills, a proven business model, and the confidence to command premium fees for transformational work.

[Profit Coach Certification Workbook](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1sIyi3JgS1ac_TmxF5s7A0DF40D74nGpWGKXLE8hNMV8/edit?usp=sharing)

**How we'll get you there:**

* **Week 1:** Master the foundation—what coaching really is and our proven COACH framework  
* **Week 2:** Develop your coaching superpowers with advanced questioning and listening techniques  
* **Week 3:** Learn to create sustainable transformation that keeps clients paying month after month  
* **Week 4:** Practice running real client sessions and earn your certification

## **Success Stories From Your Peers**

**James, Former Engineering Director:**  

*"I signed my third client at £1,950/month using someone I already knew from my corporate network. The frameworks just work."*

**Sarah, Ex-VP of Operations:**  

*"Within 6 months, I was earning more part-time coaching than I ever did climbing the corporate ladder. The freedom is incredible."*

**David, Former Finance Director:**  

*"I thought I'd need years to build credibility as a coach. The BCA certification gave me instant authority and a clear path to premium clients."*

## **Your Learning Community**

You're joining an exclusive group of high-achieving professionals who share your ambition and values. This isn't just a course—it's your entry into a community of elite coaches who support each other's success.

**During the program:** Engage with fellow participants, share insights, and build lasting professional relationships.

**After certification:** Join our alumni network where certified coaches share opportunities, celebrate wins, and continue learning together.

## **What to Expect Next**

**This week:** Get familiar with the platform, complete your welcome tasks, and prepare for Week 1.

**Week 1 (Thursday, 1st of the month 14:00-17:30 UK):** Jump straight into hands-on coaching practice with our foundational COACH model.

**Between sessions:** Practice exercises, reflect on your learning, and engage with your cohort.

## **Your Success Is Our Mission**

We don't succeed unless you succeed. Our faculty, support team, and entire program structure exist for one purpose: to ensure you leave this certification with the skills, confidence, and clarity to build the coaching practice you want.

**You've made the right decision.** Now let's make it the decision that changes everything.

## **Setting Yourself Up for Success**

You're about to invest 16 hours of focused learning over the next four weeks. The difference between participants who get certified and those who transform their careers isn't talent—it's preparation and engagement.

This lesson ensures you get maximum value from every minute.

## **Technical Setup: Get Ready to Learn**

### **Essential Platform Access**

**Zoom Requirements:**

* Download the latest Zoom app (not browser version)  
* Test your camera and microphone before Week 1  
* Ensure stable internet connection (coaching requires clear communication)  
* Have a backup connection plan (mobile hotspot, different location)

**Live Session Link:** [https://businesscoachacademy.com/calls](https://businesscoachacademy.com/calls) *Bookmark this—you'll use it every session*

### **Optimal Learning Environment**

* **Private space:** Ensure you won't be interrupted during sessions  
* **Professional setup:** You'll be on camera during practice sessions  
* **Note-taking tools:** Digital or physical—whatever works for you  
* **Water and comfort:** 3.5-hour sessions require staying focused

## **How Adults Learn Best: The Science Behind Your Success**

### **The 70-20-10 Rule**

* **70%** of learning happens through experience and practice  
* **20%** through social learning (discussions, peer coaching)  
* **10%** through formal instruction

**What this means for you:** The real learning happens when you're coaching and being coached, not just listening to content.

### **Spaced Repetition Works**

Weekly sessions with practice between allow concepts to solidify. Don't try to cram—trust the process.

### **Active Engagement Multiplies Retention**

* Participate in every exercise  
* Ask questions when confused  
* Share insights during discussions  
* Coach and be coached with enthusiasm

## **Between-Session Practice: Where Mastery Develops**

### **Week 1-2: Foundation Building**

* Practice the COACH model with colleagues, friends, or family  
* Keep it informal—"Can I ask you some questions about that challenge?"  
* Notice how different questions generate different responses  
* Journal about what you observe  
* Do more sessions on the simulator

### **Week 2-3: Skill Development**

* Record yourself asking coaching questions (audio only)  
* Listen back: Are you really listening or preparing your next question?  
* Practice power questions from your workbook  
* Find opportunities to coach rather than advise

### **Week 3-4: Real Application**

* Offer free coaching sessions to test your skills  
* Use the frameworks in your current leadership role  
* Start identifying potential coaching clients in your network  
* Document what works and what needs improvement

## **Maximizing Live Session Value**

### **Before Each Session**

* Review the previous week's workbook  
* Complete any prep exercises  
* Prepare questions about challenges you're facing  
* Set specific learning goals for the session

### **During Sessions**

* **Camera on:** You're practicing a visual skill—show up fully  
* **Participate actively:** Volunteer for demonstrations  
* **Take notes:** Capture insights, not just facts  
* **Stay present:** Resist multitasking—coaching requires full attention

### **After Sessions**

* Review and clean up your notes within 24 hours  
* Complete reflection exercises in your workbook  
* Connect with fellow participants to share insights  
* Plan your practice for the week ahead

## **Getting Support When You Need It**

### **Technical Issues**

* Test everything before your first session  
* Have backup plans ready  
* Email support immediately if problems arise (Support@businesscoachacademy.com)

### **Learning Challenges**

* Don't struggle in silence—ask questions during sessions  
* Use group discussions to clarify concepts  
* Reach out to faculty between sessions if needed

### **Peer Support**

* Connect with other participants  
* Form practice partnerships  
* Share challenges and wins  
* Build your professional network

## **Common Success Traps to Avoid**

### **The Perfectionist Trap**

Waiting until you're "ready" to practice. Start coaching from Week 1—imperfectly.

### **The Passive Learner Trap**

Thinking you can absorb skills by watching. Coaching is learned by doing.

### **The Isolated Learner Trap**

Going it alone instead of engaging with your cohort. Your peers are your greatest learning resource.

### **The Theory Trap**

Getting caught up in understanding everything intellectually before practicing. Trust the process and start doing.

## **Your Learning Commitment**

**To yourself:** Show up fully for every session, practice between weeks, and embrace the discomfort of learning new skills.

**To your cohort:** Engage authentically, support others' learning, and create a safe space for everyone to grow.

**To your future clients:** Develop the skills they deserve—because their transformation depends on your commitment to mastery.

## **What Success Looks Like**

**End of Week 1:** You understand coaching fundamentals and have practiced the COACH model.

**End of Week 2:** You're asking powerful questions naturally and see how they unlock client insights.

**End of Week 3:** You can guide meaningful transformation conversations that create lasting change.

**End of Week 4:** You're certified, confident, and ready to start earning as a professional coach.

---

**Your next step:** Complete Lesson 3 to understand your full 4-week journey, then prepare for the transformation that starts Thursday.

**Remember:** Excellence isn't about being perfect—it's about being fully engaged. Show up, practice courageously, and trust the process.

*Your coaching mastery begins with your commitment to learn.*

---

Ready for Lesson 3?

## **Your Expert Faculty Team**

### **Mark James - Chief Executive Officer**

*Your lead instructor and coaching mentor*

Mark brings over 20 years of business coaching experience to every session. He's the 'go-to' mentor for high-level coaches looking to enhance their skills, and his programs have helped hundreds of businesses globally.

**Why Mark matters to you:** He's trained thousands of professionals to become confident, skilled coaches. His practical, no-nonsense approach ensures you learn skills that actually work with real clients.

### **Pam Featherstone - 7-Figure Business Coach**

*Global #1 coach and program co-founder*

Pam is a world-renowned, multi-award-winning business coach who built a £1 million coaching business in under three years. She's coached business owners in 69 countries and holds multiple global coaching awards.

**Why Pam matters to you:** She's proof the methods work. From nearly dying from overwork to building coaching freedom—she's walked the path you're on and will show you exactly how to succeed.

## **Our Coaching Philosophy: Why BCA Is Different**

### **Coaching Is About Unlocking, Not Teaching**

Most people think coaching means giving advice or sharing expertise. We believe the client has the answers—our job is to unlock them through powerful questions and skilled listening.

**What this means for you:** You don't need to be an expert in every industry. You need to be an expert at helping people think clearly and take action.

### **Experience + Framework = Elite Coach**

You already have the credibility and business experience that takes others years to develop. We're adding proven coaching frameworks that turn your expertise into transformational conversations.

**What this means for you:** You're not starting from zero. You're adding skills to an already strong foundation.

### **Simple Systems Create Extraordinary Results**

Complex coaching models confuse coaches and clients. Our COACH and POWER frameworks are simple enough to master quickly but powerful enough to create lasting transformation.

**What this means for you:** You'll feel confident using our methods from Week 1, not months later.

### **Practice Creates Mastery**

Reading about coaching doesn't make you a coach. Practicing coaching makes you a coach. Every session includes hands-on practice with real feedback.

**What this means for you:** You'll leave feeling competent, not just informed.

## **Why We Chose the COACH Model**

### **It's Comprehensive**

The COACH model covers everything needed for a complete coaching conversation—from identifying change through to committed action.

### **It's Flexible**

Whether you're coaching for 20 minutes or 2 hours, with a CEO or team leader, the COACH model adapts to any situation.

### **It's Proven**

Our coaches use this exact framework to earn £1K-£5K per client monthly. It's not theory—it's a money-making system.

### **It Aligns with Standards**

The COACH model incorporates all ICF Core Competencies, so you're learning world-class coaching, not just our approach.

## **Our Commitment to Your Success**

### **We Don't Succeed Unless You Succeed**

Your transformation is our mission. Everything we do—from session design to ongoing support—exists to ensure you become a confident, skilled, successful coach.

### **Real Skills, Not Just Theory**

Every technique we teach has been proven in real client sessions. You're learning what actually works, not academic concepts.

### **Ongoing Support Beyond Certification**

Certification is just the beginning. You'll join an alumni community of successful coaches who support each other's growth and share opportunities.

### **Honest Feedback for Real Growth**

We'll tell you what you're doing well and what needs improvement. Our goal is your competence, not your comfort.

### **Participate Fully**

Volunteer for demonstrations, engage in discussions, and practice with enthusiasm.

### **Ask for Feedback**

We're here to help you improve. Ask for specific feedback on your coaching technique.

### **Stay Connected**

Use our support systems between sessions. Don't struggle alone.

---

**You're in expert hands.** Our faculty has guided hundreds of professionals through this exact transformation. Trust the process, engage fully, and prepare for the skills that will change your professional future.

#### **📚 Summary:**

 

This lesson introduces the concept of Day Zero and Going Pro, explaining the reasons behind the term and its significance in setting the foundation for success in business. The speaker emphasizes the need to address vulnerabilities and eliminate distractions before building a coaching business. The lesson also highlights the importance of maintaining a day one mentality and continuously striving for results. Learners are encouraged to focus on personal mastery and efficiency to increase their output and achieve their goals.

### **Going Pro Core Principles**

Notice that all these “productivity” hacks are about eliminating not adding. Whenever someone tells you “do these things that you’re not doing to have more time.” Be skeptical. Best way to make more time is to stop doing shit that doesn’t matter.

**Get More Focused Time**

Prioritise 4 hours a day to work uninterrupted. Early morning and late night work well since other people are usually clocked out and won’t bother you.

Have a block of your day that NEVER gets interrupted.Example: I don’t talk to people/take meetings until noon.That gives me 4-7hrs uninterrupted per day. Works for me.

Treat weekdays and weekends the same. Go to bed at reasonable hour. Wake up at reasonable hour. Work if you want to, it's not illegal! We just got 52-104 more days of productivity per year.

 

**Focus On What Matters**

Outsource personal activities/chores.This gives you time BACK. Cooking. Cleaning. Picking stuff up. Errands. Etc.Human-ing. You are a high-value business coach charging $500+ an hour...ACT LIKE IT!

Audit or remove meetings ruthlessly. See what stuff you can stop attending, attend less frequently, get an email update instead, or send someone in your place.

Look at your overwhelming to do list and put names next to people who can handle the items. Not all, but most, can be delegated. Then give a deadline for them. Then set calendar reminder for you to check on what you just delegated.

Stop replying to people who only ask for shit from you. Eventually, they’ll just stop.

Don’t check email until AFTER you’ve done what you need to get done.

**Environment**

Get a workspace without distractions. Notifications are the enemy. Remove all, EVERYWHERE...Your phone, your desktop, external distraction, internal distractions (deal with you limiting patterns)

Make sure you get your partner onboard by helping them understand what you are trying to achieve. You don't want a spouse to make you feel guilty for working. Cheerleaders don’t ask the quarterback to leave the game when the game’s on the line.

 

**Consumption**

Cut or Cycle stimulants. Don’t let them use you.If you can’t function without them (E.g. coffee), they’ve stopped conferring a benefit.Be able to stop them and only use them when you really know you want that extra push. On off on off is typically good enough for most, or eventually you won't need them at all.

Limit alcohol consumption. It affects sleep and mood over the next few days.It’s just a net negative from a money perspective. Not saying it’s not fun. Just saying it’s not productive. And productive is the goal.

#### **🤓 Learning Points:**

 

1. Day Zero represents a fresh start and a temporal landmark for making positive changes.  
2. Day Zero in software refers to identifying vulnerabilities that can hinder business growth.  
3. Developing a day one mentality involves prioritizing customer satisfaction and consistently pursuing results.  
4. Personal mastery and efficiency are key to building a successful coaching business.  
5. Hyperbolic discounting and "infinity pools" like social media can distract from meaningful work.

 

#### **✅ Actions & Application:**

 

1. Establish a Day Zero mindset by setting clear goals and committing to personal growth.  
2. Identify and address vulnerabilities in your business or personal life that may hinder success.  
3. Prioritize deep work and minimize distractions to increase productivity.  
4. Practice self-discipline and overcome hyperbolic discounting by allocating time for important tasks.  
5. Continuously evaluate the rewards and efforts associated with activities to make informed choices.

 

#### **⏰ Time-Stamps:**

#### **00:06: Introduction to the significance of Day Zero and Going Pro.**

00:58: Exploring the concept of Day Zero and its relation to software vulnerabilities.

01:40: Understanding the vulnerabilities that can prevent business growth.

02:32: Discussing Jeff Bezos' "always day one" mentality and its relevance to the lesson.

03:31: Highlighting the need to prepare oneself before approaching business with a day one mindset.

04:57: Emphasizing the importance of personal mastery, efficiency, and separating oneself from others.

06:30: Exploring hyperbolic discounting and the impact of distraction on productivity and goal achievement.

08:42: Encouraging learners to evaluate their effort and subjective value of activities to make intentional choices.

* [How To Trick Your Brain Into Falling Asleep](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A5dE25ANU0k)

 

### **Summary:**

Hey there! Are you looking to boost your energy and take your performance to the next level? Well, look no further! In this video, we're diving deep into the world of energy and how it can impact your success. We'll uncover the secrets of fasting, explore the body's physiology and emotions, and reveal the dangers of being in a negative emotional state. Plus, we'll share valuable tips on improving your sleep quality and fueling your body for optimal performance. So, get ready to unleash your inner energy powerhouse and watch this video now!

 

### **Learning Points:**

1. The benefits of fasting on mental and physical performance.  
2. How the body's physiology influences emotions and the importance of being aware of it.  
3. Understanding the difference between negative and positive emotions and their energy levels.  
4. The impact of chronic inflammation on motivation and decision-making.  
5. The importance of quality sleep and tracking it to improve performance.  
6. How chronic inflammation affects the brain's mindset and inhibits long-term work efforts.  
7. The role of nutrition in fueling your body for success.  
8. Creating awareness of activities that give you energy and those that drain you.

### **Actions and Behaviors:**

1. Experiment with fasting to find a fasting window that works best for you.  
2. Cultivate awareness of your emotions and the physiological cues behind them.  
3. Prioritize high-quality sleep and explore tools like the Aura Ring or Bed It Sleep Monitor.  
4. Focus on reducing chronic inflammation through lifestyle changes (e.g., exercise, stress management, and a healthy diet).  
5. Set long-term goals and maintain focus on them, rather than succumbing to impulsive behavior.  
6. Pay attention to the quality of your fuel by incorporating nutritious foods into your diet.  
7. Create a list of energy-boosting activities and reduce or eliminate energy-draining activities.  
8. Develop rapid recovery rituals for physical, emotional, mental, and purpose energy.

 

Remember, by implementing these actions and behaviors, you'll unlock your true energy potential and set yourself up for unparalleled success. So, don't wait any longer – dive into this video now and start your pro energy journey today!

### **Resources**

* [Time Worksheets](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/12Q3RIddya5qi0XJVouinS3rdvxKRp02SBHMf-PEYCy8/edit?usp=sharing)  
* [Setting Up Time Tracking In Harvest](https://app.businesscoachacademy.com/lesson/setting-up-time-tracking-in-harvest/)

 

#### **📚 Summary:**

 

In this lesson, you'll learn how to effectively manage your time to increase productivity and success. Discover the benefits of efficient time management and its impact on your personal and professional life. As Albert Einstein once said, "The only reason for time is so that everything doesn't happen at once."

 

#### **🤓 Learning Points:**

 

1. Importance of time management: Understand why managing your time effectively is crucial for success.  
2. Meal preparation and delivery: Learn how to save time by outsourcing food preparation.  
3. Minimizing email usage: Discover why sending unnecessary emails is a time-waster and how to avoid it.  
4. Distractions and interruptions: Understand the negative impact of distractions and how to minimize them for better focus and productivity.  
5. Productivity systems and tools: Explore practical methods and tools for effective time management.  
6. Prioritizing tasks: Learn how to prioritize tasks based on value and alignment with your goals.  
7. Avoiding non-business social media activities: Discover the negative effects of excessive social media usage and how to avoid wasting time on it.  
8. Creating strategic blocks of time: Understand the importance of uninterrupted time for strategic thinking and innovation.  
9. Morning and evening routines: Learn the significance of setting up your day and recovering from work with consistent routines.  
10. Maximizing recovery: Discover the importance of allocating time for recovery and aligning it with important activities.

 

#### **✅ Actions & Application:**

 

1. Utilize meal delivery services or prepare meals in advance to save time on cooking.  
2. Minimize unnecessary emails and focus on high-value tasks.  
3. Implement strategies to minimize distractions and interruptions during work.  
4. Explore productivity systems and tools such as task management apps or time trackers.  
5. Prioritize tasks based on their value and alignment with your goals.  
6. Avoid excessive social media usage for non-business purposes.  
7. Create designated blocks of uninterrupted time for strategic thinking and innovation.  
8. Establish consistent morning and evening routines to set up for success and recover effectively.  
9. Allocate separate time for recovery that aligns with important activities.  
10. Maximize productivity by eliminating activities that do not contribute to recovery or important tasks.

#### **📚 Summary:**

 

In this video, we explore the importance of focus and how it can lead to success in achieving our goals. We learn how to prioritize tasks, eliminate distractions, and harness our energy to maximize productivity. By implementing the strategies discussed, we can improve our ability to concentrate, make better use of our time, and ultimately achieve greater success.

 

#### **🤓 Key Learning Points:**

 

1. The power of focus: Understand the impact of focus on achieving goals and the importance of aligning goals, energy, and time availability.  
2. Prioritize tasks: Learn how to prioritize tasks based on their importance and urgency to optimize productivity.  
3. Eliminate distractions: Discover practical ways to reduce distractions like social media, TV, and unnecessary clutter in order to maintain focus.  
4. Dopamine reset: Discover the concept of dopamine fasting to rewire the brain for better focus and overcome instant gratification habits.  
5. Enhance concentration: Explore different techniques and tools, such as focus mode, to enhance concentration during work or study sessions.

 

#### **✅ Actions & Application:**

 

1. Prioritize effectively: Start prioritizing tasks by importance and urgency to ensure that your time and energy are invested in tasks that yield the best results.  
2. Eliminate distractions: Take steps to reduce or eliminate distractions such as decluttering your environment and setting specific times for email and social media usage.  
3. Dopamine fasting: Gradually reduce dependence on instant gratification triggers to improve your ability to focus for extended periods of time.  
4. Use focus-enhancing tools: Explore tools like focus mode and extensions that can enhance concentration and minimize distractions during work or study sessions.

 

#### **⏰ Time-Stamps:**

 

00:00 Introduction

01:20 The power of focus

03:15 Prioritize tasks

04:45 Eliminate distractions

06:32 Dopamine reset

08:10 Enhance concentration

 

Remember, by implementing these strategies and techniques consistently, you can become more focused and achieve your goals more efficiently.

#### **📚 Summary:**

 

This lesson is all about pro productivity and maximizing efficiency in your work. The speaker shares their personal system for consistent and high-value activity. They introduce various software tools like Evernote, Trello, and Sunsama, explaining how each one can be used to manage different aspects of productivity. The lesson emphasizes the importance of having clear goals, prioritizing tasks, and time blocking.

 

#### **🤓 Learning Points:**

 

1. The importance of having written goals with specific objectives, motivations, and potential obstacles.  
2. How to use Evernote for capturing notes, content, and setting life goals.  
3. Using Trello as a central hub for project management and creating a roadmap towards your goals.  
4. Incorporating Sunsama for task management, time blocking, and integrating with Google Calendar.  
5. Utilizing Google Drive for file storage and organizing larger files and media.  
6. Implementing Harvest for time tracking and project management integration.

 

#### **✅ Actions & Application:**

 

1. Create written goals with specific objectives, motivations, and potential obstacles.  
2. Explore and set up Evernote as a tool for capturing notes, content, and setting life goals.  
3. Utilize Trello as a central hub for project management and creating a roadmap towards your goals.  
4. Experiment with Sunsama for task management, time blocking, and integrating with Google Calendar.  
5. Organize files and media using Google Drive and link them to tasks in Trello.  
6. Consider using Harvest for time tracking and project management integration.

 

#### **⏰ Time-Stamps:**

 

00:06: Introduction to pro productivity and the importance of consistent work.

01:02: Exploring the "going pro system" and the use of written goals.

01:47: Introduction to Evernote for capturing notes, content, and setting life goals.

02:59: Introducing Trello as a project management tool for creating a roadmap.

03:38: Integration of Sunsama for task management, time blocking, and calendar integration.

04:20: Overview of Google Calendar for default diary and easy appointment management.

05:04: Utilizing Google Drive for file storage and media organization.

05:49: Overview of Harvest for time tracking and project management integration.

06:32: Demonstration of the speaker's personal system and software setup.

#### **📚 Summary:**

 

In this lesson, we explore the concept of developing a pro mindset for personal and professional growth. We learn about the factors that influence motivation and how to manage them effectively. The lesson emphasizes the power of visualization and the role of mindset in achieving success. By aligning our mindset and taking consistent action, we can overcome obstacles and reach our goals.

 

#### **🤓 Learning Points:**

 

1. Motivation is defined as commitment divided by environment.  
2. Factors that influence motivation include confidence, importance, self-efficacy, value, impulsiveness, and delay.  
3. The endowment effect and loss aversion impact motivation.  
4. Visualizing a new reality can change beliefs and lead to progress.  
5. Building confidence and self-efficacy through performance accomplishments is key.  
6. Having a clear framework and checklist helps remove confusion and stagnation.  
7. Earning a high income, freedom, and positive impact motivate growth and change.

 

#### **✅ Actions & Application:**

 

1. Practice visualizing your goals and desired outcomes to reinforce belief and motivation.  
2. Break down your goals into manageable tasks and create a checklist to stay on track.  
3. Focus on building your confidence through previous successes and learning from others' achievements.  
4. Take consistent action, regardless of fear or discomfort, to move towards your goals.  
5. Embrace the mindset of progress and constantly seek ways to improve and learn.

 

#### **⏰ Time-Stamps:**

 

06:32 - Understanding motivation and the procrastination equation

11:45 - Factors influencing commitment and environment

18:20 - The power of visualization and mindset in achieving success

24:55 - Building confidence and self-efficacy through performance accomplishments

30:15 - Utilizing a clear framework and checklist for progress

35:40 - Benefits of earning a high income, freedom, and positive impact on others

### **What Is This?**

This lesson will guide you through the process of choosing the most suitable business structure for your coaching practice, focusing on Sole Trader and Limited Liability Company (LLC or Ltd) options.

### **Why Is This Important?**

Choosing the right business structure will help you:

* **Protect against liabilities:** Understand your personal risk.  
* **Optimize taxation:** Ensure you are taxed appropriately.  
* **Manage administrative overhead:** Understand the complexity of running your chosen structure.

Legally we cannot advise on business structures, you need to speak with your accountants. However, the majority of our coaches trade as a limited company in the UK or LLC in the USA.

### **Business Structure Comparison**

| Aspect | Sole Trader | Limited Liability Company (LLC or Ltd) |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Definition** | A sole trader is an individual running a business on their own. | A separate legal entity from its owners. |
| **Liability** | Personal liability for all business debts and obligations. | Personal assets are protected from business liabilities. |
| **Taxation** | Simplified tax process; profit is taxed as personal income. | Separate tax entity; potential tax benefits and complexities. |
| **Administrative Overhead** | Easier and less costly to set up and run. | More complicated and costly to set up and maintain. |

### **Steps to Choose Your Business Structure**

1. **Evaluate Your Needs:**  
   * Assess your need for liability protection.  
   * Consider tax implications and benefits.  
   * Think about the administrative burden you can handle.  
2. **Utilize Government and Professional Resources:**  
   * Visit government websites for definitions and implications of each structure.  
   * Use professional advisory sites to get a comprehensive understanding.

---

### **Consult Recommended Professionals**

**UK:**

* **Accountants:** Seek advice on whether to choose Sole Trader or Ltd.  
* **Resources:** HMRC website, professional advisory organizations.

**USA**

* **Tax Advisors:** Get insights into LLC structuring and implications at state and federal level.  
* **Resources:** IRS website, Small Business Administration (SBA).

### **Practical Tips**

* **Document Your Findings:** Keep notes on pros and cons relevant to your situation.  
* **Ask Specific Questions:** In consultations, ask about specific scenarios or concerns.  
* **Plan for the Future:** Consider how your business might grow and how that affects your choice.

### **Summary**

Choosing the correct business structure is not a big decision and can be changed later if needed. DO NOT let it hold up you making progress through the Academy. By researching your options and consulting with professionals, you can make an informed decision that protects you from liabilities, optimises your taxes, and balances administrative demands.

This sets you up for long-term success and enables you to focus on what you do best: coaching your clients to achieve their goals.

### **What Is This?**

This lesson guides you through the process of setting up a business bank account to manage your finances effectively.

### **Why Is This Important?**

Having a dedicated business bank account will help you:

* Separate your personal and business finances.  
* Ensure clear and transparent financial records.  
* Facilitate easy tracking of business income and expenses.  
* Maintain professionalism and build trust with clients and vendors.

Legally we cannot advise on which banks to use. However at Business Coach Academy we use **Revolut** (and have a referral code which gets you reduce rates)

### **Steps to Set Up a Business Bank Account**

#### **Research Banking Options**

| Aspect | Description | Why It's Important |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Identify Banks** | Look for banks offering business accounts with beneficial features for small businesses, such as low fees, online banking, and good customer service. | Choosing the right bank can save you money and provide better financial management tools for your business. |
| **Compare Features** | Compare the features and benefits of different business accounts, such as account fees, transaction limits, and additional services. | Ensures you select an account that best meets your business needs and offers the most value. |

#### **Prepare Documentation**

| Required Documentation | Description | Why It's Important |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Identification** | Passport, driver's license, or another form of personal identification. | Proof of identity is required for anti-money laundering regulations and to open any bank account. |
| **Business Registration Documents** | Certificates of incorporation, partnership agreements, or other official documents. | Confirms your business's legal status and structure, which is required to open a business bank account. |
| **Proof of Address** | Utility bills, lease agreements, or other documents showing your business address. | Verifies your business's physical location, which is typically required by banks. |

#### **Engage with Banking Specialists**

| Action | Description | Why It's Important |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Book Consultations** | Schedule meetings with business banking specialists to understand the best practices and steps. | Provides personalized advice on setting up and managing your business bank account efficiently. |
| **Ask Questions** | Inquire about account management practices, fees, and additional services that could benefit your business. | Helps you make informed decisions and utilize banking services to support your business growth. |

### **Summary**

Setting up a dedicated business bank account is vital for effective financial management. By researching banking options, preparing the necessary documentation, and consulting with banking specialists, you can ensure a smooth and efficient account setup process. This will help you maintain clear financial records, facilitate easy tracking of income and expenses, and support the professional growth of your coaching business.

### **What Is This?**

This guide covers the essential insurance policies you need to protect your coaching business against claims and liabilities.

### **Why Is This Important?**

Securing the right insurance will help you:

* Protect against potential claims and liabilities.  
* Ensure your business is compliant with legal requirements.  
* Provide peace of mind for you and your clients.

**Legally we cannot recommend what insurance is right for your business.**

### **Understanding Business Insurance**

#### **Essential Insurance Policies**

| Type of Insurance | Definition | Why It's Important |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Professional Indemnity Insurance** | Covers claims against your business for professional negligence or mistakes. | Protects against financial losses due to claims of inadequate service or advice. |
| **Public Liability Insurance** | Covers claims made by members of the public for accidents or injuries that occur on your business premises. | Protects against legal and compensation costs if someone is injured or their property is damaged. |
| **Employers’ Liability Insurance** | Required if you have employees; covers claims from employees who are injured or become ill while working for you. | Protects against compensation costs and legal fees from employee claims. |

### **Steps to Secure Business Insurance**

1. **Research Insurance Needs:**  
   * **Professional Indemnity:** Essential for any business offering professional advice or services.  
   * **Public Liability:** Important if you interact with the public or clients in person.  
   * **Employers’ Liability:** Mandatory if you employ staff.  
2. **Consult with Insurance Brokers:**  
   * **BCA Recommendations:** Consult with the Business Coach Academy team for the latest recommendations on trusted insurance brokers.  
   * **Get Quotes:** Obtain multiple quotes to compare coverage options and costs.  
3. **Understand Policy Details:**  
   * Review the terms, conditions, and exclusions of each policy.  
   * Ensure the coverage limits are adequate for your business needs.  
4. **Regularly Review Your Coverage:**  
   * As your business grows, periodically review and update your insurance policies to ensure continued adequate protection.

### **Summary**

Securing the right business insurance is crucial for protecting your coaching practice from potential claims and liabilities. By understanding the different types of insurance available and consulting with professionals, you can ensure that your business is well-protected and compliant with legal requirements.

### **What Is This?**

This lesson covers the key tax obligations and considerations for your chosen business structure, focusing on both the UK and USA.

### **Why Is This Important?**

Understanding tax implications will help you:

* Stay compliant with tax laws.  
* Optimise your tax strategy.  
* Avoid unexpected tax liabilities.  
* Make informed decisions about your business finances.

We legally cannot give tax advice but we can recommend people if you wish.

### **Key Tax Obligations**

#### **🇬🇧 UK Tax Obligations**

| Tax Type | Description | Why It's Important |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Value Added Tax (VAT)** | A tax on the value added to goods and services, applicable if your turnover exceeds the VAT threshold (at time of writing 90K in 12 month period) | Ensures compliance with tax laws and avoids penalties for underpayment. |
| **Corporation Tax** | A tax on the profits of a limited company. | Required for all limited companies to remain compliant with UK tax laws. |
| **Salary vs. Dividends** | Distinction between drawing a salary and taking dividends from your company’s profits. | Different tax treatments can impact your overall tax liability and financial planning. |

#### **🇺🇸 USA Tax Obligations**

| Tax Type | Description | Why It's Important |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **Federal and State Taxes** | Income taxes applicable at both federal and state levels. | Ensures you meet all your tax obligations and avoid penalties. |
| **Self-Employment Tax** | Taxes for self-employed individuals to cover Social Security and Medicare contributions. | Necessary for compliance and securing future Social Security and Medicare benefits. |
| **S Corporation Benefits** | Potential tax benefits available if you elect to be taxed as an S Corporation. | Can minimize your overall tax liability through salary and dividend distributions. |

### **Steps to Understand and Manage Tax Implications**

1. **Research Tax Obligations:**  
   * **UK:** Understand VAT, Corporation Tax, and the distinctions between salary and dividends.  
   * **USA:** Learn about federal and state tax obligations, self-employment tax, and the benefits of S Corporation status.  
2. **Consult with Professionals:**  
   * **Schedule Consultations:** Book meetings with accountants or tax advisors to clarify tax strategies and obligations specific to your business.  
   * **Tailored Advice:** Get advice tailored to your specific business structure and financial situation.  
3. **Implement Tax Strategies:**  
   * **Optimize Tax Payments:** Use the information and advice from professionals to minimize your tax liabilities.  
   * **Stay Organized:** Keep accurate records and documentation to ensure smooth tax filing and audits.  
4. **Regular Reviews:**  
   * **Update Strategies:** Periodically review and update your tax strategies as your business grows or tax laws change.  
   * **Ongoing Consultations:** Maintain regular communication with your tax advisor to stay informed about any new tax regulations or opportunities.

### **Summary**

Understanding the tax implications of your business structure is crucial for staying compliant and optimizing your tax strategy. By researching tax obligations, consulting with professionals, and implementing effective tax strategies, you can ensure your business remains financially healthy and compliant with all tax laws.

### **How to Conduct an Effective Meeting**

 

**Developing Agendas**

Develop the agenda together with key participants in the meeting. Think of what overall outcome you want from the meeting and what activities need to occur to reach that outcome. 

The agenda should be organized so that these activities are conducted during the meeting. Allocate a time limit for each topic in the agenda, and try to stick to this time to make sure everything can get covered. 

Design the agenda so that participants get involved early by having something for them to do right away and so they come on time.

Next to each major topic, include the type of action needed, the type of output expected (decision, vote, action assigned to someone), and time estimates for addressing each topic.

Don’t be afraid to shift the agenda during the course of the meeting. Sometimes topics are covered in a much shorter time than anticipated, or tpics could need more attention than the original time allowed.

 

**Opening Meetings**

Always start on time; this respects those who showed up on time and reminds late-comers that the scheduling is serious.

Welcome attendees and thank them for their time.

Review the agenda at the beginning of each meeting, giving participants a chance to understand all proposed major topics, change them and accept them.

 

**Establishing Ground Rules for Meetings**

Four powerful ground rules are:

1. Participate  
2. Get focus  
3. Maintain momentum  
4. Reach closure

List your primary ground rules on the agenda. Go through the gorund rules during the first meeting of every year, or when you have new members participating. 

 

**Time Management**

One of the most difficult facilitation tasks is time management. It is really easy to get caught up in a discussion and veer off topic. Keep to the point as much as possible, and if longer discussions need to be had schedule another meeting with the specific poeple who need to be involved.

Try to stick to the time allocated to each section in the agenda. If time runs out before you have finished, perhaps move a less important agenda section to the next meeting

 

**Leave meetings with clear next steps and owners**

Some effective meeting strategies to help ensure that people leave your meeting with clarity and purpose include:

* Summarise the meeting with notes and action items. Have one person dedicated to taking notes each week, or alternate the note taking between team members.    
* Notes should then be sent to all participants after each meeting.   
* Assign action items or things to follow up on to specific individuals whenever possible. It’s also helpful to schedule a deadline or a time when someone will check in on progress.

### **Recruitment Process: Topgrading**

The goal of recruiting is attracting and hiring A Players at all levels of the organisation, is as critical as landing the right customers.

This requires the active participation of the marketing function in the recruiting process and the use of the Topgrading methodology in the interviewing and selection process.

Topgrading has a proven track record of hiring the right right leaders more than 90% of the time vs the 25-30% success rate achieved with feel good conversations, testing or standard behaviour based approaches.

It is the best method to find the right candidates who fit your differentiated strategy and culture, to scale up the business.

How To Get The Best talent

 

**STEP 1: Create Job Scorecard**

Before starting your search for a key executive or frontline associate, create a Job Scorecard (vs. the standard job description). A Job Scorecard details a person’s:

1. Purpose for the job  
2. The desired outcomes of this individual’s work- specific and measurable outcomes a potential hire needs to accomplish over the coming 1-3 years.  
3. The competencies required to execute it  
   * technical  
   * cultural- It’s more important to hire for this kind of fit than for specific skills, so long as a person has the capacity to learn and grow.

While a job description tends to list what people will be doing, a Job Score-card describes the outcomes you want from such activities.

* $8 million in revenue  
* 7 new FTSE 100 clients  
* 100% contract renewal rate amount members  
* Being specific about outcomes allows you to directly evaluate each candidate’s capacity to actually deliver these results.

 

**STEP 2: Attract the largest and most capable talent pool**

You must attract a large number of capable people excited about the job and willing to accept your compensation package (e.g., McDonald’s has the same chances of hiring A Players as Goldman Sachs).

You also need to have the tools to select the best people from this group of prospects. This is the purpose of the Job Scorecard in Step 1.

These central elements of the Job Scorecard — the outcomes and competencies — drive the recruiting, interviewing, and selection process. If you want to have a differentiated strategy, you can’t hire, compensate, and have the same HR systems supporting the same people as your competition.

You need a “strange” culture and a “strange” strategy to differentiate your firm in the marketplace. This is why it’s so critical to discern the real Core Values underpinning your culture; and create the elements of an industry-dominating strategy.

**Recruiting Is a (Guerilla) Marketing Function**

* The best people to consider first are those with whom you have already worked.  
* The marketing team must be as actively involved in recruiting a steady stream of potential employees as it is in attracting potential customers, yet most organizations fail miserably in this area.  
* The key to finding the right people is by creating a recruitment strategy that reflects your Core Values and Purpose and then using your marketing skills to reach the right potential pool of talent.  
* It is easy to stand out if you use whatever approach fits your culture and you show some originality.

 

**STEP 3: Pre-Screen Candidates With A Work History Form**

Before any interviews occur, recruiters should send candidates a detailed work history questionnaire to fill out. This helps identify and remove weak candidates early in the process, preserving resources needed for interviews later on.

Work history forms usually ask for information such as salary history, manager feedback, reasons for leaving previous jobs, and a self-appraisal of past performance.

 

**STEP 4 & 5: Topgrading Interview & Rock Solid Evaluation process**

Once you have attracted a large number of qualified candidates, which significantly increases your chances of finding A Players, a rock-solid evaluation process is needed to bring the numbers down.

First stage is to go from 20 to 10, then to three, and finally to the one top candidate who will deliver 150% of your Job Scorecard.

1. A Screening Interview The screening interview is used to further narrow your list from 10 to the top three candidates. This interview consists of five powerful screening interview questions- [see types of questions here](https://www.lever.co/blog/topgrading-interview-guide/). The questions work for any position for which you’re hiring and can be addressed in 30 to 45 minutes over the phone or in a short meeting.  
2. Chronological In-Depth Structured (CIDS) interview This is what you do once you’re down to the top three candidates, it’s a thorough three- to four-hour interview covering a candidate’s entire career history. The objective is discovering behavioural and performance patterns that are likely to repeat themselves at your workplace. When hiring, bear in mind that past performance is the best indicator of future performance. A three- to four-hour interview is appropriate for a middle or senior leader; one to two hours might be more suitable for entry-level or less experienced candidates with limited work history. Checking for culture fit in the interview process can be handled in a variety of ways. 

 

**STEP 6: Reference Call** s

During the process the candidate is informed that a final step in hiring is for the candidate to arrange calls with their managers (past and current); the reference call with the current manager will be performed when the candidate agrees to arrange it

 

**EXTRA STEP: Test-Drive Potential Employees** 

Though it’s not always possible, the best way to select the right people is to have candidates work with you for several weeks.

### **Define Your Strategic Intent**

A vital part of running your business is clearly defining your intent and goals. What are you trying to achieve and is it actually achievable?

When looking at your mission statement you need to ask yourself if you can answer these questions:

1. Does it allow the team to know exactly what they are  trying to do and what they are not trying to do?  
2. Will you know when you are done?

If your mission statement can clearly answer these two questions, it provides clarity and focus to your business and your intent. 

Mission statements are valuable, because they are what inspires the team and gives you direction for your business.

### **Resources**

* [Strategic Intent](https://gregmckeown.com/read-one-platitude-filled-mission-statement-ill-scream/)

### **Keep Your Talent Engaged**

Google used its capabilities of analysing data to try and figure out how to apply the decisions it makes in response to engineering challenges, to decisions regarding people.

The conclusion that was reached was that periodic one on one coaching (rather than superior technical knowledge) ranked as the #1 key to being a successful leader. 

Managers should focus those coaching sessions on five topics representing the five main activities of successful managers.  

 

**Here are the five in reverse order:**

 

**5. Hire fewer people, but pay them more.**

By having fewer employees with better salaries, you will have a team that will be loyal to your business. They will want to work hard if they know they are being fairly compensated. 

 

**4. Give recognition, and show appreciation.**

In order for people to be productive in their workplace, they need to experience a positive work environment. As a manager, you can provide this by having positive interactions with your employees that include praise and recognition for the work they do.

 

**3. Set clear expectations, and give employees a clear line of sight.**

Take time to explain to your employees how the work they do contributes to the overall success of the business.

If employees understand how their role fits into the bigger picture, they will feel more secure in their work and more determined to provide good work.

 

**2. Don’t demotivate; “dehassle.”** 

The best managers are less concerned about motivating their people and more concerned about NOT demotivating them.

Try and find ways to make each of your employee’s jobs as easy for them as possible, and work with them to eliminate things that cause them major stress or demotivate them. 

 

**1. Help people play to their strengths** 

If people are doing jobs that they are not good at, they will feel discouraged and unmotivated. While if they are working at things they excel in they will be more productive and will produce a higher quality of work. 

* Help individual employees focus more on activities that give them strength and less on activities that make them weak.    
* Embrace strengths-based management practices, as this will bring you more fulfilled, happier, and engaged employees who will lift themselves and your organization to new levels of energy and performance.

### **Accountability, Responsibility, and Authority**

These business terms are often used interchangeably, but it is important to know the various distinctions.

**Accountability:** 

This belongs to the ONE person. This individual should be tracking the progress and notify the business when issues arise within a defined task, team, function, or division. The rule: If you have more than one person accountable, for a task, then if something goes wrong the blame will get shifted from person to person.

**Responsibility:**

This includes anyone who is involved in a certain part of the business, and who knows how a specific project should end up. 

**Authority:**

The person or team that is in charge, or who make the final decisions will have authority. Leaders are responsible for bridging the gap between accountability and authority.

### **Function Accountability**

Function Accountability focuses on making sure you have employed the right people to take leadership positions in your business. You can determine if these people are fit for the job by asking these two questions:

**1. Do they need to be managed?**

**2. Do they regularly wow the team with their insights and output?**

If you need to bring someone in from the outside to fill a senior leadership position, you should do this only once every six to nine months. The rule is to take it slow when bringing outsiders into senior leadership roles. Unless your company is growing at a rapid pace and you need to fill 4-5 key leadership roles within a very limited amount of time. 

**Completing Your Function Accountability** 

 

**STEP 1**

List the functions your business must support. Focus on the jobs that need to get done.  

 

**STEP 2**

Once the team has agreed on the people accountable for each function, consider these four questions: 

**1.** Do you have more than one person accountable for a function? The rule is that only one person should be accountable; otherwise, there will be confusion.

**2.** Does someone’s name show up in more than everyone else’s? 

**3.** Do you have any functions with no person accountable for them?  

**4.** Are you enthusiastic about the person you have chosen for each function?    

 

**STEP 3** 

For the next step list one to three KPIs for each of the listed functions. These are intended to be leading indicators, measuring the daily and weekly activities of a particular leader and meant to drive superior results.  

 

**STEP 4**

You will then need to pull out a recent detailed Profit and Loss (P& L) and Balance Sheet (B/ S) statement and assign a person accountable for each line item. 

Then discuss the same four questions we asked earlier about the people listed for the functions. Try to spread the workload evenly among the leadership team, with just one person ultimately accountable for each item.

### **Process Accountability**

Process Accountability lists who is accountable for each of the four to nine processes that drive the business and how each process will be measured to guarantee it’s running smoothly.  

We strongly recommend that companies implement the principles of Lean. Lean is an approach to process design focused on eliminating time wasted on activities that don’t add value to customers.  

 

**Completing Your Process Accountability Chart (PACe)** 

**STEP 1** 

Get your team together and name the four to nine key processes driving the business.  This can take a couple of 90-minute sessions to complete.  

 

**STEP 2**

Next, assign accountability for each process to a specific person. Remind everyone that 

although someone is taking accountability for a process, it doesn’t mean that they are the new boss of the process, nor do they necessarily have increased decision making authority.

Their job is to monitor the process (time, cost, quality), let the team know if there are any issues, and lead a regular meeting to fix or improve that particular process. 

 

**STEP 3**

Identify a few KPIs to track each process. 

One of the most important KPIs for processes is time in either number of days or number of hours. Although time is not the only KPI, it does drive efficiency in your business and customer satisfaction and is the key measurement in the Lean process of designing and streamlining processes.
